prompt:
## Intent

Generate a test for the login page

## Desired Outcome

Generate a Playwright test suite for the login page within the intent-poc-app to verify rendering and interaction.

## Repo Context

- Intent Driven Development POC (intent-poc-app)
- Start in Surface Library: src/demo-app/render/render-surface-frame.ts and src/demo-app/render/render-surface-page.ts.
- If the owner is adjacent, check src/demo-app/model/catalog.ts and src/demo-app/model/types.ts next.
- intent-poc-app verification note: Stateful UI bugs should declare and verify the requested UI state before screenshot evidence is trusted.
- intent-poc-app verification note: Prefer reusable state activation contracts over prompt-specific routing or one-off checkpoint logic.
- intent-poc-app verification note: Theme-sensitive UI bugs require paired light-mode and dark-mode screenshot evidence; do not accept a dark-mode-only capture set.
- intent-poc-app repo note: Use this source for prompt-planning, Studio changes, and built-in surface library evidence.
- intent-poc-app repo note: Surface library screenshot regeneration routes through this source with an explicit library publish opt-in.
- Open routing question: Prompt normalization identified Surface Library as the most likely code surface within intent-poc-app.

## Source Scope

- intent-poc-app
- Selected source: intent-poc-app.
- Likely owning surface: Surface Library (medium confidence).
- Fallback surfaces to inspect next: Intent Studio.

## Adaptive Boundaries

- Focus on page-level rendering and interaction
- Verify theme-mode consistency (light/dark)
- Keep this draft inside intent-poc-app until the owning surface is confirmed.
- Start with Surface Library before widening into adjacent surfaces.
- Resolve whether the change belongs to Intent Studio before touching multiple UI surfaces.

## Minimum Success

- Test suite executes successfully
- Login page renders without console errors
- Screenshot evidence captured for both light and dark themes
- Generate a Playwright test suite for the login page within the intent-poc-app to verify rendering and interaction.
- The draft keeps the change centered on Surface Library instead of the whole repo.
- The final plan names one existing UI verification path instead of inventing a new workflow.

## Baseline

- Existing surface library page rendering patterns
- Capture the current baseline in Surface Library before changing behavior.

## Verification Obligations

- Use theme-mode activation contract
- Include paired light/dark mode screenshot evidence
- Name the existing Playwright or screenshot check that will prove the behavior change.

## Raw Intent

Generate a test for the login page

Generate a test for the login page

Desired outcome: Generate a Playwright test suite for the login page within the intent-poc-app to verify rendering and interaction.

alternatives.reason: Prompt normalization kept this as a fallback interpretation.

businessIntent.statement: Generate a Playwright test suite for the login page within the intent-poc-app to verify rendering and interaction.
businessIntent.desiredOutcome: A functional Playwright test suite that validates the login page UI rendering and user interaction flow within the intent-poc-app.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The test suite verifies the presence of username and password input fields.
The test suite verifies the login button is clickable and triggers a submission state.
The test suite includes visual regression snapshots for both light and dark theme modes.

scenarios.title: Verify Login Page Rendering
scenarios.goal: Ensure the login page renders correctly in both themes.
given: The user navigates to the login page
when: The Playwright test suite executes in light mode
The Playwright test suite executes in dark mode
then: The login page elements are visible
Visual snapshots match the expected baseline for both themes

scenarios.title: Verify Login Interaction
scenarios.goal: Ensure user input and submission work as expected.
when: The user clicks the login button
then: The UI reflects the expected post-submission state

workItems.title: Verify Login Page UI and Interaction
workItems.description: Validate that the login page renders correctly in both light and dark themes and that user credentials can be submitted.
workItems.userVisibleOutcome: The login page renders correctly and accepts user credentials.
workItems.verification: Playwright test suite with visual regression and interaction checks.

specs.testName: Verify Login Page Rendering and Interaction

requiredUiStates.label: Theme mode
requiredUiStates.description: The demo app supports light and dark theme states that materially change contrast, tokens, and screenshot evidence.

notes: The demo app now honors the dark query parameter across Studio and library routes for deterministic verification.
The library surface toggle remains available for manual verification flows.
Intent Studio keeps an explicit toggle for interactive review during manual sessions.
Stateful UI bugs should declare and verify the requested UI state before screenshot evidence is trusted.
Prefer reusable state activation contracts over prompt-specific routing or one-off checkpoint logic.
Theme-sensitive UI bugs require paired light-mode and dark-mode screenshot evidence; do not accept a dark-mode-only capture set.
Verify the active theme state before trusting screenshot comparisons.
Prefer the shared dark query parameter during generated verification so screenshots start in the requested theme.
Dark-theme verification should keep a light-mode reference screenshot alongside the dark-mode target evidence.
requiredUiStates.reason: Prompt requests the Theme mode UI state with value "light".

checkpoints.assertion: Username and password fields are visible.

destinations.label: Controller artifacts
destinations.reason: Local evidence bundles are always written by the controller.
details: Stores plan, manifests, logs, captures, and summaries on disk.

destinations.label: Source workspace publication
destinations.reason: Workspace publishing is disabled until artifacts.storageMode is set to 'both'.
details: Useful when evidence should live beside the source repository.

tools.label: BDD planning
tools.reason: The system converts the raw prompt into reviewable acceptance criteria and scenarios during the full reviewed plan pass.
details: Deterministic by default, with optional Gemini refinement when configured.

tools.label: Playwright TDD generation
tools.reason: Applicable sources now produce Playwright-first executable test plans and checkpoint screenshots.
details: Generated specs are intended for checked-in repo storage with overwrite semantics.

tools.label: Visual verification
tools.reason: Applicable sources currently expose visual checkpoints and capture definitions.
details: Playwright screenshots remain one execution tool rather than the top-level product model.

tools.label: Environment deployment
tools.reason: A generic local or Kubernetes deployment stage is planned but not yet wired into the runner.
details: This will become the shared path for local, minikube, and k3s-backed execution.

tools.label: Implementation loop
tools.reason: The runner can execute bounded implementation attempts against the prepared source workspace.
details: QA failures can feed directly back into the next bounded implementation attempt.

tools.label: QA verification
tools.reason: The runner can now execute a bounded QA bundle before evidence capture completes the lane.
details: The default QA bundle runs typecheck, the deterministic code test suite, and generated Playwright specs when a source enables them.

tools.label: Evidence reporting
tools.reason: Every run produces summaries and machine-readable manifests.
details: Reports become distribution inputs for downstream review and publishing surfaces.
